an1.31:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see even a single other _dhamma_ that, when so untamed, leads to such great harm as this: mind [*citta*].
an1.31:1.2: Bhikkhus, an untamed mind [*citta*] leads to such great harm.”
an1.32:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see even a single other _dhamma_ that, when so tamed, leads to such great benefit as this: mind [*citta*].
an1.32:1.2: Bhikkhus, a tamed mind [*citta*] leads to such great benefit.”
an1.33:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see even a single other _dhamma_ that, when so unguarded, leads to such great harm as this: mind [*citta*].
an1.33:1.2: Bhikkhus, an unguarded mind [*citta*] leads to such great harm.”
an1.34:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see even a single other _dhamma_ that, when so guarded, leads to such great benefit as this: mind [*citta*].
an1.34:1.2: Bhikkhus, a guarded mind [*citta*] leads to such great benefit.”
an1.35:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see even a single other _dhamma_ that, when so unprotected, leads to such great harm as this: mind [*citta*].
an1.35:1.2: Bhikkhus, an unprotected mind [*citta*] leads to such great harm.”
an1.36:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see even a single other _dhamma_ that, when so protected, leads to such great benefit as this: mind [*citta*].
an1.36:1.2: Bhikkhus, a protected mind [*citta*] leads to such great benefit.”
an1.37:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see even a single other _dhamma_ that, when so unrestrained, leads to such great harm as this: mind [*citta*].
an1.37:1.2: Bhikkhus, an unrestrained mind [*citta*] leads to such great harm.”
an1.38:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see even a single other _dhamma_ that, when so restrained, leads to such great benefit as this: mind [*citta*].
an1.38:1.2: Bhikkhus, a restrained mind [*citta*] leads to such great benefit.”
an1.39:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see even a single other _dhamma_ that, when so untamed, unguarded, unprotected, and unrestrained, leads to such great harm as this: mind [*citta*].
an1.39:1.2: Bhikkhus, an untamed, unguarded, unprotected, and unrestrained mind [*citta*] leads to such great harm.”
an1.40:1.1: “Bhikkhus, I do not see even a single other _dhamma_ that, when so tamed, guarded, protected, and restrained, leads to such great benefit as this: mind [*citta*].
an1.40:1.2: Bhikkhus, a tamed, guarded, protected, and restrained mind [*citta*] leads to such great benefit.”
